title: Will Is From Mars

jokes.text: Come on, Ash, it's not the size that counts.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks how many carats the engagement ring has
jokes.punchline: Will's earnest deflection sounds like sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre setup works well but is fairly predictable given the ring size question

jokes.text: That small, huh?
jokes.setup: Will's deflection about size not mattering
jokes.punchline: Ashley immediately calls out that it must be tiny
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that cuts through Will's BS with maximum economy

jokes.text: I couldn't afford the real cubic zirconia.
jokes.setup: Will admits he didn't get the ring he wanted
jokes.punchline: He couldn't afford 'real' cubic zirconia - fake diamonds
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - can't afford fake diamonds, perfect character moment showing Will's financial reality vs. his swagger

jokes.text: Nothing says loving like something from the oven.
jokes.setup: Will explains Geoffrey baked the ring in a cookie
jokes.punchline: Riffs on Pillsbury advertising slogan inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Mild commercial parody works but feels a bit forced and predictable

jokes.text: And Will Smith says it best.
jokes.setup: Will just quoted a commercial slogan
jokes.punchline: He credits himself as Will Smith the actor, breaking fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Classic Fresh Prince meta-humor where Will acknowledges his real identity, signature move of the show

jokes.text: What y'all watching, a Rosie Perez movie?
jokes.setup: Sped-up voices are playing on TV
jokes.punchline: References Rosie Perez's distinctive high-pitched voice
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about voice characteristics, timely reference for 1995

jokes.text: Hilary gave us a tape of her talk show and made us promise to watch. It's been on for three minutes, and we're almost done.
jokes.setup: They're watching Hilary's talk show tape on fast-forward
jokes.punchline: Three minutes of sped-up viewing covers almost her entire show
jokes.explanation: Perfect character burn on Hilary's vapid talk show, economical setup and payoff

jokes.text: It's that thong underwear you bought me for Christmas.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what's bothering Will
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable admission about intimate gift in front of family
jokes.explanation: Solid awkward oversharing moment that plays on Christmas gift expectations

jokes.text: My living room. Thanks to you, my apartment is flooded.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests a midnight swim seductively
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's because Will flooded her apartment
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed misdirection that subverts romantic expectation with practical reality

jokes.text: Tell her, y'all. Didn't I just fix the toaster?
jokes.setup: Will defends his handiness after flooding Lisa's apartment
jokes.punchline: Points to toaster as evidence of his skills
jokes.explanation: Sets up the visual gag but is just setup itself, gains power from the payoff

jokes.text: See? Before it wouldn't even get past the door.
jokes.setup: Toaster dings and presumably moves or launches itself
jokes.punchline: Will's 'fix' made the toaster mobile/autonomous
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd visual where Will's repair made appliance sentient/mobile, perfect physical comedy

jokes.text: Thanks, Will. In the morning, I'll set it on dark and it'll meet me at school.
jokes.setup: The toaster apparently can move around autonomously now
jokes.punchline: Ashley matter-of-factly describes using the mobile toaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan callback that extends the absurd logic - treating mobile toaster as normal convenience

jokes.text: I love a good old-fashioned sleepover. What say we rent a musical... pop some corn and after that, dare I say, Yahtzee?
jokes.setup: Carlton offers Lisa to stay in the pool house
jokes.punchline: His idea of a wild sleepover is wholesome board games and musicals
jokes.explanation: Pure Carlton character comedy - his sheltered, earnest approach to what he thinks is rebellious fun

jokes.text: Dare I say: Ow!
jokes.setup: Carlton enthusiastically suggests Yahtzee
jokes.punchline: Will physically reacts in pain to Carlton's lameness
jokes.explanation: Standard physical reaction to Carlton's uncoolness, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: It was, 20 years ago.
jokes.setup: Hilary compliments Geoffrey's 'new' suit
jokes.punchline: Deadpan admission it's decades old
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey deadpan delivery, perfect timing and character consistency

jokes.text: Your wit, ha-ha-ha... your charm... your wit. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Hilary is buttering up Geoffrey to work for her
jokes.punchline: She repeats 'wit' because she can't think of other qualities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - her vapid manipulation attempt shows her limited vocabulary

jokes.text: Oh, what do you call that big loud thing that sucks up everything?
jokes.setup: Hilary can't remember the word for vacuum cleaner
jokes.punchline: Sets up for Geoffrey's dad joke
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the daddy joke, works but is fairly predictable

jokes.text: You call him 'Daddy.'
jokes.setup: Hilary describes something that 'sucks up everything'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey applies it to Phil's appetite/greed
jokes.explanation: Classic dad joke structure executed well, plays on Phil's known appetite and dominance

jokes.text: I'm expected to be on call for his 3 a.m. feeding.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey explains he can't moonlight because Phil needs him
jokes.punchline: Describes Phil like an infant with night feedings
jokes.explanation: Brilliant characterization that infantilizes Phil while showing Geoffrey's sardonic wit

jokes.text: The extra money could get you that Beemer.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey resists working for Hilary after Phil's generosity
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately tempts him with material desires
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary move - she immediately appeals to materialism over loyalty

jokes.text: Well, it's not as if he gave her a kidney.
jokes.setup: Phil paid for Geoffrey's mother's cataract surgery
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey minimizes Phil's generosity with medical comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Geoffrey rationalizing betrayal, good character moment

jokes.text: [IN SPANISH ACCENT] Hey, Lucy, I home.
jokes.setup: Will enters the pool house
jokes.punchline: Does Ricky Ricardo impression from I Love Lucy
jokes.explanation: Generic pop culture impression without much setup or payoff, feels dated

jokes.text: Girl, exactly what part of that chicken are you cooking?
jokes.setup: Will smells Lisa's chicken stew
jokes.punchline: Implies the smell is so bad it must be an unappetizing part
jokes.explanation: Standard bad cooking joke, setup works but punchline is predictable

jokes.text: [COUGHING] Oh, damn, I swallowed it!
jokes.setup: Will reluctantly tastes Lisa's cooking
jokes.punchline: He accidentally swallows the engagement ring hidden in food
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the proposal setup, physical comedy with real consequences

jokes.text: Oh, and it was finger-licking good, baby.
jokes.setup: Will just swallowed an engagement ring
jokes.punchline: He covers with KFC slogan despite obvious distress
jokes.explanation: Good recovery line that maintains the charade while being ironic given the situation

jokes.text: As lovely as that stinks... Uh, uh, smells.
jokes.setup: Phil politely declines dinner invitation
jokes.punchline: He accidentally says what he really thinks about the smell
jokes.explanation: Classic Freudian slip showing Phil's inability to completely hide his thoughts

jokes.text: Note: Buy Reese's Pieces for desk jar.
jokes.setup: Phil gives them what appears to be a business card
jokes.punchline: Will reads the wrong side with a random personal note
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual gag showing Phil's absent-mindedness

jokes.text: Yeah. But I could damn sure go for some Reese's Pieces.
jokes.setup: Discussion about therapy needs
jokes.punchline: Will fixates on the candy from Phil's note instead
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Will's priorities and short attention span

jokes.text: Look here, Uncle Phil, I'm from Philly. You know what I mean? I was driving when I was 11, you know. Of course, that was in the good old days before The Club.
jokes.setup: Phil says you need lessons before driving
jokes.punchline: Will reveals he was stealing cars as a child before anti-theft devices
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment revealing Will's street background while being darkly humorous about childhood crime

jokes.text: [LISA & WILL GRUNT] Hey, baby, what's Swahili for 'ching-ching'?
jokes.setup: Phil offers to pay for honeymoon anywhere in the world
jokes.punchline: They immediately get excited about money and ask about Swahili
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their immediate motivation change when money is involved

jokes.text: Hey, you know them little yellow smiley faces? Just put some Hershey syrup on them, you got us, you know.
jokes.setup: Will says they're very happy
jokes.punchline: Compares them to chocolate-covered smiley faces
jokes.explanation: Quirky visual metaphor that's mildly funny but a bit forced

jokes.text: Oh, if you mean what I think you mean, I got skills, you know.
jokes.setup: Therapist mentions interpersonal relationship skills
jokes.punchline: Will thinks he's talking about sexual abilities
jokes.explanation: Predictable sexual misunderstanding, works but is fairly basic

jokes.text: Listen, Rita, you are sucking me dry. Look, you want more alimony, you talk to my first two wives, all right?
jokes.setup: Marriage counselor takes call during session about relationships
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been divorced three times and is bitter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - marriage counselor is terrible at marriage, undermines his authority perfectly

jokes.text: Never get married on a dare. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Therapist just revealed multiple failed marriages
jokes.punchline: Casually admits one marriage was just from a dare
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd marriage motivation, great character moment

jokes.text: You know. But first, I figure we'd start out with Will, Jr., you know. Then Willfred, then Willoughby, then Willard, you know. Then Willis.
jokes.setup: Will talks about having kids like the Huxtables
jokes.punchline: Plans to name all children variations of 'Will'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Will's narcissism through baby naming

jokes.text: 'Will Stop'? That ain't no good name.
jokes.setup: Lisa tells Will to stop listing names
jokes.punchline: Will treats 'Will Stop' as another potential name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that extends the naming joke while showing Will's obsession

jokes.text: Now, Willstafa, you know. That's strong, you know, like, kind of a Lion King kind of feel. You know, Will Simba.
jokes.setup: Will continues naming children after himself
jokes.punchline: Combines 'Will' with 'Mustafa' and 'Simba' from Lion King
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the naming joke with timely Lion King reference, though getting repetitive

jokes.text: Cleveland. / Philly.
jokes.setup: Therapist asks where they'll live
jokes.punchline: They immediately give different answers simultaneously
jokes.explanation: Classic relationship comedy setup showing they haven't discussed basics

jokes.text: It's a pool table.
jokes.setup: Hilary wants Geoffrey to carry a table to her car
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey matter-of-factly points out the impossibility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan response to Hilary's oblivious demands

jokes.text: Duh. Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey points out it's a pool table
jokes.punchline: Hilary acts like that was obvious despite her unreasonable request
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary character moment showing her inability to admit mistakes

jokes.text: I don't believe blacktopping your tennis court falls into that category.
jokes.setup: Hilary dismisses last night's work as 'little yard work'
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals she made him pave her tennis court
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing the absurd extent of Hilary's exploitation

jokes.text: You are so lazy.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey just described paving her entire tennis court
jokes.punchline: Hilary calls him lazy for complaining about it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - Hilary's complete disconnect from reality about work

jokes.text: Not too lazy to quit.
jokes.setup: Hilary calls him lazy
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey threatens to quit with typical understated delivery
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey comeback with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: That was seven hours ago.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey said he'd be right back after getting fresh air
jokes.punchline: He's been gone for seven hours
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that reveals Geoffrey's passive-aggressive behavior

jokes.text: Aw, don't get into a hissy fit, sir. I left dinner in the oven.
jokes.setup: Phil complains about Geoffrey being gone for seven hours
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey dismisses it and mentions dinner as if that fixes everything
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey deflection with condescending 'sir' and minimal effort

jokes.text: God help us. You'd think a guy who eats so much... would at least know his way around the kitchen.
jokes.setup: They're relieved Geoffrey made dinner instead of Phil
jokes.punchline: Ashley points out the irony of Phil's eating vs. cooking ability
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Phil's eating habits vs. cooking skills

jokes.text: Will who?
jokes.setup: Phil asks where Will is
jokes.punchline: Lisa pretends not to know Will after their fight
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective way to show their relationship is in serious trouble

jokes.text: Man, we are this close to breaking up. Boy, that stuff doesn't work at all.
jokes.setup: Phil asks if there was a problem in therapy
jokes.punchline: Will reveals therapy made everything worse
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for relationship comedy, predictable outcome

jokes.text: Oh, don't hurt me!
jokes.setup: Lisa approaches with food
jokes.punchline: Will dramatically cowers in fear
jokes.explanation: Standard physical overreaction to bad cooking, predictable but functional

jokes.text: Did you put something on or did you take something off?
jokes.setup: Will is confused by Lisa's appearance
jokes.punchline: He can't tell if she's dressed up or down
jokes.explanation: Mild relationship comedy that shows their disconnect, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Trust me, the next time I decide to take something off... you will be too old and blind to care.
jokes.setup: Will's confused question about her appearance
jokes.punchline: Lisa threatens he'll never see her naked again
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with dark humor about their relationship's future

jokes.text: Hey, put it there, dude.
jokes.setup: Will apologizes and they seem to make up
jokes.punchline: He offers a handshake instead of intimacy to his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Will's emotional distance after their fight

jokes.text: So you expect me to be a gourmet chef, raise eight kids... and look beautiful 24 hours a day?
jokes.setup: They're discussing future roles in marriage
jokes.punchline: Lisa lists Will's unrealistic expectations
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship comedy highlighting gender role expectations

jokes.text: Too much?
jokes.setup: Lisa lists his completely unrealistic expectations
jokes.punchline: Will mildly questions if it might be excessive
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response showing Will's obliviousness to how ridiculous his expectations are

jokes.text: That one-figure salary that you pull down at the Peacock?
jokes.setup: Will says he'll support the family
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals his job pays almost nothing
jokes.explanation: Good reality check that deflates Will's provider fantasy

jokes.text: And it ain't gonna be me, Aunt Jemima!
jokes.setup: Will starts to be mature about their fight
jokes.punchline: He immediately insults Lisa with a racist stereotype
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Will can't maintain maturity, though the insult feels dated

jokes.text: Look here. I sit when and where I please. You know what I'm saying? Because I'm the man. I'm the man.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggests they sit together
jokes.punchline: Will makes it about masculine dominance
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's toxic masculinity issues but isn't particularly funny

jokes.text: Because your name is on the seat.
jokes.setup: Will demands a reason to sit where she suggests
jokes.punchline: Simple practical reason deflates his masculine posturing
jokes.explanation: Good deflation that shows the practical reality undermining Will's posturing

jokes.text: I knew that. And you know how I knew that? Because you're the man.
jokes.setup: Will realizes his name is on the seat
jokes.punchline: He tries to save face by claiming credit for Lisa's observation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's inability to admit he was wrong

jokes.text: Get the lead out, Weezy. / I'm walking as fast as I can, George.
jokes.setup: Older couple enters therapy session
jokes.punchline: They're clearly based on George and Louise Jefferson
jokes.explanation: Direct reference to The Jeffersons characters, nostalgic but not particularly creative

jokes.text: That's, uh... That's Dr. Whitehorn. / Yeah, yeah, Horny.
jokes.setup: Doctor corrects his name
jokes.punchline: George reduces it to inappropriate nickname
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual pun that fits George's crude character

jokes.text: Never mind. I'll sit wherever I want, because I'm the man. I'm the man.
jokes.setup: George dismisses seating directions
jokes.punchline: Will immediately copies the toxic masculine attitude
jokes.explanation: Shows Will learning bad habits from George, but repetitive

jokes.text: Your mother died. Your mother is dead. Your mother ain't living no more.
jokes.setup: Asked to name three things he likes about Louise
jokes.punchline: George lists the same morbid 'positive' three different ways
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and unexpected - instead of praising his wife, he celebrates her mother's death in escalating ways

jokes.text: [IMITATING WILL] Who the man? I'm the man.
jokes.setup: Lisa does role reversal exercise to show Will
jokes.punchline: She immediately captures his annoying catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good character exercise showing how annoying Will's behavior looks from outside

jokes.text: Because I ain't spending no 4.95 on some stank skeezer... to take her to the salad bar.
jokes.setup: Lisa continues imitating Will
jokes.punchline: She reveals his cheap dating standards
jokes.explanation: Good character roasting showing Will's cheapness and poor treatment of women

jokes.text: [IMITATING LISA] Tsk. Tsk. Tsk.
jokes.setup: Will's turn to imitate Lisa
jokes.punchline: He focuses on her signature disapproving tongue-click
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy capturing Lisa's disapproving mannerism

jokes.text: Why you talking about your uncle all the time? He ain't fat. He is big-boned.
jokes.setup: Will continues imitating Lisa's complaints
jokes.punchline: She defends Phil with common euphemism for overweight
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke using common euphemism, fits character dynamics

jokes.text: And I thought I had it bad. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will and Lisa's imitations show their toxic relationship
jokes.punchline: George realizes his marriage looks good by comparison
jokes.explanation: Good perspective moment showing relative dysfunction levels

jokes.text: And this is for Mama. / And this is for calling me Weezy... when you know I'm sensitive about my asthma.
jokes.setup: Louise attacks George with foam bats
jokes.punchline: She lists specific grievances while hitting him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows Louise getting revenge for earlier insult

jokes.text: No, she used to be mean. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Louise has always been difficult
jokes.punchline: George implies she's gotten worse over time
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan escalation showing their relationship has deteriorated

jokes.text: We tied the knot 40 years ago and I've been swinging from it ever since.
jokes.setup: George explains their long marriage
jokes.punchline: Transforms marriage metaphor into hanging/suicide imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay that transforms innocent marriage metaphor into suicidal imagery

jokes.text: Yeah, I hear you. Sure, there's women like that... make you realize why God made darkness.
jokes.setup: Will bonding with George over difficult women
jokes.punchline: Will's dark comment about preferring darkness
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that escalates the misogynistic bonding moment

jokes.text: You insulting my wife? / Hold up a second there, cul-de-sac.
jokes.setup: George gets offended at Will's agreement about difficult wives
jokes.punchline: Will calls him 'cul-de-sac' as insult
jokes.explanation: Mildly creative insult playing on George's suburban lifestyle

jokes.text: Look, I am twice your size... and half your age. You need to chill out a little bit.
jokes.setup: Will responds to George's challenge
jokes.punchline: Uses superior size and youth as intimidation
jokes.explanation: Standard tough guy posturing, sets up the escalation

jokes.text: Your daddy is so fat that when he went to school... he sat next to everybody.
jokes.setup: George starts a 'your daddy' joke battle
jokes.punchline: Classic fat joke with clever spatial logic
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed fat joke with good visual absurdity

jokes.text: He looks like a car coming down the road with both doors open.
jokes.setup: Louise insults Will's big ears
jokes.punchline: Creative visual metaphor comparing ears to open car doors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor that creates a perfect mental image, highly quotable

jokes.text: You might as well be taking karate.
jokes.setup: Everyone is fighting instead of communicating
jokes.punchline: Therapist sarcastically suggests they're learning violence instead
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation about therapy gone wrong

jokes.text: No, we used bats. / On each other? / Oh, no, no. We beat up this old couple.
jokes.setup: Phil asks if they had a fistfight
jokes.punchline: Will reveals they ganged up on elderly couple instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts expectation of fighting each other

jokes.text: We wanna miss the rainy season in the motherland.
jokes.setup: They explain moving up their wedding
jokes.punchline: Reveals Phil is sending them to Africa for expensive honeymoon
jokes.explanation: Reveals they chose an expensive African destination, shows their priorities

jokes.text: That's from the travel agent. She says she could retire on this one. Ha-ha-ha.
jokes.setup: They're going to Africa for honeymoon
jokes.punchline: Travel agent can retire from the commission
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing just how expensive Phil's generosity is

jokes.text: Pinch me, Geoffrey.
jokes.setup: Phil realizes how much he's spending on their honeymoon
jokes.punchline: He wants Geoffrey to wake him from the nightmare
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for Geoffrey's final punchline

jokes.text: Not on what you're paying me. But for 35,000, I'll slap you silly.
jokes.setup: Phil asks Geoffrey to pinch him
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey offers violence for the honeymoon money instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey closer - mercenary, specific dollar amount, and perfect escalation

tmdb_overview: Thanks to relationship counseling, Will and Lisa hop to move on up to a new understanding in preparation for marriage. Meanwhile Hillary wants Geoffrey to work at her new home.
